title: The Riddle Riddle: Testing Flexible Reasoning in Large Language Models and Humans
abstract: arXiv:2606.27103v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Humans flexibly adapt their reasoning strategies to the requirements of a given problem. Large language models (LLMs) have performed well on many cognitive tasks, however, it is unclear whether this accuracy is a result of pattern matching from training data or flexible reasoning. Here, we introduce a novel paradigm to test this question: the riddle riddle paradigm. Riddle riddles are word problems written to mimic popular riddles, but altered so their answers only require literal interpretations. Identifying correct answers requires looking past the structure of each question and flexibly apply different reasoning strategies based on the content. If LLMs respond to surface features, such as form, a riddle-like structure should cause models to use an inventive reasoning strategy even when a literal interpretation suffices. Alternatively, if LLMs reason based on content, they should flexibly switch strategies when appropriate. Across two

translated_title: 谜题谜题：测试大型语言模型与人类中的灵活推理能力
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.27103v1 公告类型：新论文 摘要：人类能够灵活调整其推理策略以适应特定问题的要求。大型语言模型（LLMs）在许多认知任务上表现良好，然而，尚不清楚这种准确性是源于训练数据的模式匹配还是灵活推理。为此，我们引入了一种新颖的测试范式：谜题谜题范式。谜题谜题是模仿流行谜语编写的文字问题，但其答案仅需字面解释即可得出。识别正确答案需要超越每个问题的结构，并根据内容灵活应用不同的推理策略。如果LLMs对表面特征（如形式）做出反应，那么即使字面解释足够，谜语般的结构也会促使模型使用创造性推理策略。反之，如果LLMs基于内容进行推理，它们应在适当情况下灵活切换策略。我们通过两个实验进行了测试。
tldr: 该研究通过“谜题谜题”新范式，测试大型语言模型能否基于内容灵活调整推理策略，而非仅依赖表面模式匹配。
en_tdlr: This study introduces the "riddle riddle" paradigm to test whether large language models can flexibly adjust reasoning strategies based on content, rather than relying solely on surface pattern matching.
